<user_query>
can you add this to remote positions?
https://joblet.ai/jobs?employmentType=Remote 
</user_query>
I'll check how remote aggregator sources are registered, then add joblet.ai.

arguments.query: aggregator seeds remote employment type remote positions sources

content.text: Remote sources need a seed plus an ATS scraper. I'll inspect how Remote OK / Remote DXB are wired and what joblet.ai exposes.

input.command:
curl -sI "https://joblet.ai/jobs?employmentType=Remote" | head -40; echo "---"; curl -s "https://joblet.ai/jobs?employmentType=Remote" | head -c 8000; echo; echo "---API probes---"; for u in \
  "https://joblet.ai/api/jobs?employmentType=Remote" \
  "https://joblet.ai/api/v1/jobs?employmentType=Remote" \
  "https://api.joblet.ai/jobs?employmentType=Remote" \
  "https://joblet.ai/jobs.json?employmentType=Remote" \
  "https://joblet.ai/sitemap.xml" \
  "https://joblet.ai/robots.txt"; do
  echo "=== $u ==="
  curl -sI -L --max-time 10 "$u" | head -15
  echo
done
input.description: Probe joblet.ai HTML and API endpoints

content.text: `/api/jobs` exists (HEAD returned 405). Digging into the real GET payload and how board fetchers register.

input.command:

echo
echo "=== GET with page ==="
curl -sS -L --max-time 20 \

  -H "Referer: https://joblet.ai/jobs?employmentType=Remote" \

echo
echo "=== robots ==="
curl -sS "https://joblet.ai/robots.txt"
echo
echo "=== JS search for api paths ==="
curl -sS "https://joblet.ai/_next/static/chunks/app/jobs/page-8416fe633d5f4ee6.js?REDACTED" -o /tmp/joblet_jobs_page.js
rg -o '.{0,40}/api/[^"'\''`]{0,80}' /tmp/joblet_jobs_page.js | head -40
rg -o 'employmentType[^"'\''`]{0,60}|remote[^"'\''`]{0,40}|jobs\?[^"'\''`]{0,80}' /tmp/joblet_jobs_page.js | head -40
wc -c /tmp/joblet_jobs_page.js

python3 <<'PY'
import json, urllib.request
url = "https://joblet.ai/api/jobs?employmentType=Remote&page=1&limit=20"
req = urllib.request.Request(url, headers={
    "Accept": "application/json",
    "User-Agent": "Mozilla/5.0",
    "Referer": "https://joblet.ai/jobs?employmentType=Remote",
    "Origin": "https://joblet.ai",
})
with urllib.request.urlopen(req, timeout=30) as r:
    data = json.load(r)
print("top keys:", data.keys())
print("data keys:", data["data"].keys())
d = data["data"]
for k,v in d.items():
    if k != "jobs":
        print(f"  {k}: {v}")
jobs = d["jobs"]
print("jobs count:", len(jobs))
# employment types / categories sample
from collections import Counter
et = Counter()
cat = Counter()
remoteish = 0
for j in jobs:
    for e in j.get("employmentType") or []:
        et[e]+=1
    cat[j.get("category") or ""] += 1
    ets = [e.lower() for e in (j.get("employmentType") or [])]
    if "remote" in ets:
        remoteish += 1
print("employmentType:", et)
print("category:", cat)
print("remote count:", remoteish)
print("sample keys:", sorted(jobs[0].keys()))
print("sample job:", {k: jobs[0][k] for k in ["id","title","location","applyUrl","url_slug","category","employmentType"]})
print("company:", jobs[0].get("company"))
PY

# probe pagination / category params
for q in \
  "employmentType=Remote&page=1&pageSize=50" \
  "employmentType=Remote&page=1&perPage=50" \
  "employmentType=Remote&offset=0&limit=50" \
  "employmentType=Remote&category=Technology+%26+IT" \
  "employmentType=Remote&category=AI" \
  "employmentType=Remote&industry=Technology+%26+IT" \
  "employmentType=Remote&q=engineer" \
  "employmentType=Remote&search=software"; do
  echo "=== ?$q ==="
  python3 -c "
import json,urllib.request,sys
url='https://joblet.ai/api/jobs?$q'
req=urllib.request.Request(url,headers={'Accept':'application/json','User-Agent':'Mozilla/5.0','Referer':'https://joblet.ai/jobs?employmentType=Remote','Origin':'https://joblet.ai'})
try:
  with urllib.request.urlopen(req,timeout=20) as r:
    d=json.load(r)['data']
  meta={k:v for k,v in d.items() if k!='jobs'}
  print('n=',len(d.get('jobs') or []), 'meta=',meta)
except Exception as e:
  print('ERR',e)
"
done
input.description: Inspect joblet API pagination and filters

input.command:
python3 <<'PY'
import json, urllib.request, urllib.parse
from collections import Counter

def fetch(params):
    q = urllib.parse.urlencode(params, doseq=True)
    url = f"https://joblet.ai/api/jobs?{q}"
    req = urllib.request.Request(url, headers={
        "Accept": "application/json",
        "User-Agent": "Mozilla/5.0",
        "Referer": "https://joblet.ai/jobs?employmentType=Remote",
        "Origin": "https://joblet.ai",
    })
    with urllib.request.urlopen(req, timeout=30) as r:
        return json.load(r)

# page through Technology & IT remote with cursor
params = {"employmentType": "Remote", "category": "Technology & IT", "limit": 50}
seen = set()
remote_tech = []
pages = 0
cursor = None
while pages < 5:
    p = dict(params)
    if cursor:
        p["cursor"] = cursor
        # also try nextCursor as page token variants
    data = fetch(p)["data"]
    pages += 1
    jobs = data.get("jobs") or []
    pag = data.get("pagination") or {}
    print(f"page={pages} n={len(jobs)} pag={pag}")
    for j in jobs:
        jid = j.get("id")
        if jid in seen:
            continue
        seen.add(jid)
        ets = [e.lower() for e in (j.get("employmentType") or [])]
        if "remote" not in ets:
            continue
        if (j.get("category") or "") != "Technology & IT":
            continue
        remote_tech.append(j)
    cursor = pag.get("nextCursor")
    if not pag.get("hasMore") or not cursor:
        break

print("unique remote tech:", len(remote_tech))
print("employers sample:", Counter((j.get("company") or {}).get("name") for j in remote_tech).most_common(10))
# try page=2 with category
for page in (1,2,3):
    data = fetch({"employmentType":"Remote","category":"Technology & IT","page":page,"limit":50})["data"]
    jobs = data.get("jobs") or []
    remote = [j for j in jobs if "Remote" in (j.get("employmentType") or [])]
    print(f"page param {page}: n={len(jobs)} remote={len(remote)} ids0={jobs[0]['id'] if jobs else None}")
PY

# Download main chunks and search for api/jobs usage
cd /tmp
curl -sS "https://joblet.ai/jobs?employmentType=Remote" -o joblet.html
# extract chunk URLs
rg -o '/_next/static/chunks/[^"]+\.js' joblet.html | sort -u > chunks.txt
wc -l chunks.txt
# fetch larger chunks that might contain API client
mkdir -p joblet_chunks
while read p; do
  f=$(basename "$p" | cut -d'?' -f1)
  curl -sS "https://joblet.ai${p}" -o "joblet_chunks/$f" 2>/dev/null || true
done < chunks.txt
# search for employmentType / api/jobs patterns
rg -l 'api/jobs|employmentType|nextCursor|/api/' joblet_chunks | head -20
rg -o '.{0,80}api/jobs.{0,120}' joblet_chunks/* 2>/dev/null | head -30
rg -o '.{0,60}employmentType.{0,80}' joblet_chunks/* 2>/dev/null | head -40
rg -o 'perPage.{0,40}|limit.{0,40}|cursor.{0,40}' joblet_chunks/app* 2>/dev/null | head -40
input.description: Mine joblet JS for API client params

echo '===='
rg -a -l 'joblet.ai/api' .
rg -a -l 'fetchJobs|searchJobs|getJobs' .
# strings containing Remote
rg -a --no-filename -o '.{0,40}Remote.{0,60}' 8897*.js 3049*.js 7766*.js 2>/dev/null | head -40
# check RSC payload / flight data
curl -sS -H 'RSC: 1' -H 'Next-Router-State-Tree: %5B%22%22%2C%7B%22children%22%3A%5B%22jobs%22%2C%7B%22children%22%3A%5B%22__PAGE__%22%2C%7B%7D%5D%7D%5D%7D%2Cnull%2Cnull%2Ctrue%5D' \
  'https://joblet.ai/jobs?employmentType=Remote' | head -c 3000
echo
# try Next data
curl -sS 'https://joblet.ai/jobs?employmentType=Remote&_rsc=1' | head -c 2000
echo
# try graphql?
for u in \
  'https://joblet.ai/api/search?employmentType=Remote' \
  'https://joblet.ai/api/job-search?employmentType=Remote' \
  'https://joblet.ai/api/jobs/search?employmentType=Remote' \
  'https://joblet.ai/api/jobs/remote' \
  'https://joblet.ai/api/public/jobs?employmentType=Remote'; do
  code=$(curl -sS -o /tmp/jout -w '%{http_code}' -H 'Accept: application/json' -H 'Origin: https://joblet.ai' "$u")
  echo "$code $u $(head -c 120 /tmp/jout | tr '\n' ' ')"
done

python3 <<'PY'
import json, urllib.request, urllib.parse
from collections import Counter

def get(path, params):
    q = urllib.parse.urlencode(params, doseq=True)
    url = f"https://joblet.ai{path}?{q}" if params else f"https://joblet.ai{path}"
    req = urllib.request.Request(url, headers={
        "Accept": "application/json",
        "User-Agent": "Mozilla/5.0",
        "Referer": "https://joblet.ai/jobs?employmentType=Remote",
        "Origin": "https://joblet.ai",
    })
    with urllib.request.urlopen(req, timeout=30) as r:
        body = json.load(r)
    return url, body

# /api/search variants
for params in [
    {"q": "software engineer", "employmentType": "Remote"},
    {"query": "software", "employmentType": "Remote"},
    {"q": "engineer", "employmentType": "Remote", "limit": 50},
    {"q": "python", "employmentType": "Remote", "category": "Technology & IT"},
]:
    try:
        url, body = get("/api/search", params)
        data = body.get("data") or body
        jobs = data.get("jobs") if isinstance(data, dict) else None
        meta = {k:v for k,v in (data or {}).items() if k!='jobs'} if isinstance(data, dict) else None
        print("SEARCH", params, "n=", len(jobs or []), "meta=", meta)
        if jobs:
            et=Counter(tuple(j.get("employmentType") or []) for j in jobs)
            print("  et", et.most_common(5))
            print("  sample", jobs[0].get("title"), jobs[0].get("employmentType"), (jobs[0].get("company") or {}).get("name"))
    except Exception as e:
        print("SEARCH ERR", params, e)

print("--- jobs/search ---")
for params in [
    {"employmentType": "Remote", "limit": 50},
    {"employmentType": "Remote", "q": "software", "limit": 50},
    {"employmentType": "Remote", "category": "Technology & IT", "limit": 50},
    {"isRemote": "true", "limit": 50},
    {"remote": "true", "limit": 50},
    {"workType": "Remote", "limit": 50},
]:
    try:
        url, body = get("/api/jobs/search", params)
        data = body.get("data") or {}
        jobs = data.get("jobs") or []
        meta = {k:v for k,v in data.items() if k!='jobs'}
        rem = sum(1 for j in jobs if "Remote" in (j.get("employmentType") or []))
        print("JS", params, "n=", len(jobs), "remote=", rem, "pag=", meta.get("pagination"))
        if jobs:
            print("  sample", jobs[0].get("title"), jobs[0].get("employmentType"), jobs[0].get("location"))
    except Exception as e:
        print("JS ERR", params, e)

# POST?
import urllib.error
for path in ["/api/jobs", "/api/search", "/api/jobs/search"]:
    payload = json.dumps({"employmentType":"Remote","limit":20,"category":"Technology & IT"}).encode()
    req = urllib.request.Request(f"https://joblet.ai{path}", data=payload, method="POST", headers={
        "Accept":"application/json","Content-Type":"application/json",
        "Origin":"https://joblet.ai","Referer":"https://joblet.ai/jobs?employmentType=Remote",
        "User-Agent":"Mozilla/5.0",
    })
    try:
        with urllib.request.urlopen(req, timeout=20) as r:
            body = json.load(r)
        data = body.get("data") or {}
        jobs = data.get("jobs") or []
        print("POST", path, "n=", len(jobs), "keys=", list(data)[:8])
    except urllib.error.HTTPError as e:
        print("POST", path, e.code, e.read()[:200])
PY

python3 <<'PY'
import json, urllib.request, urllib.parse
from collections import Counter

def fetch(params):
    q = urllib.parse.urlencode(params, doseq=True)
    url = f"https://joblet.ai/api/jobs/search?{q}"
    req = urllib.request.Request(url, headers={
        "Accept": "application/json",
        "User-Agent": "Mozilla/5.0",
        "Referer": "https://joblet.ai/jobs?employmentType=Remote",
        "Origin": "https://joblet.ai",
    })
    with urllib.request.urlopen(req, timeout=30) as r:
        return json.load(r)["data"]

# page through remote, collect categories
cats = Counter()
titles = []
seen = set()
for page in range(1, 6):
    data = fetch({"employmentType": "Remote", "page": page, "limit": 50})
    jobs = data.get("jobs") or []
    pag = data.get("pagination")
    print(f"page={page} n={len(jobs)} pag={pag}")
    if jobs:
        print("  first id", jobs[0]["id"], "last", jobs[-1]["id"])
    for j in jobs:
        if j["id"] in seen: continue
        seen.add(j["id"])
        cats[j.get("category") or ""] += 1
        if (j.get("category") or "") in ("Technology & IT", "AI"):
            titles.append((j.get("title"), (j.get("company") or {}).get("name"), j.get("location"), j.get("applyUrl","")[:60]))

print("categories:", cats.most_common())
print("tech/ai count in 5 pages:", len(titles))
print("samples:", titles[:15])

# try pageSize / perPage on search
for params in [
    {"employmentType":"Remote","page":1,"perPage":50},
    {"employmentType":"Remote","page":1,"pageSize":50},
    {"employmentType":"Remote","page":1,"limit":100},
]:
    data = fetch(params)
    print("size probe", params, "n=", len(data.get("jobs") or []), "pag=", data.get("pagination"))

# category + remote on search endpoint again with page
data = fetch({"employmentType":"Remote","category":"Technology & IT","page":1,"limit":50})
jobs = data.get("jobs") or []
print("cat+remote n=", len(jobs), "remote=", sum(1 for j in jobs if "Remote" in (j.get("employmentType") or [])), "pag=", data.get("pagination"))
print("cats", Counter(j.get("category") for j in jobs))
print("sample", [(j["title"], j.get("employmentType"), (j.get("company") or {}).get("name")) for j in jobs[:8]])
PY

python3 <<'PY'
import json, urllib.request, urllib.parse
from collections import Counter

def fetch_jobs(params):
    q = urllib.parse.urlencode(params, doseq=True)
    url = f"https://joblet.ai/api/jobs?{q}"
    req = urllib.request.Request(url, headers={
        "Accept": "application/json",
        "User-Agent": "Mozilla/5.0",
        "Referer": "https://joblet.ai/jobs?employmentType=Remote",
        "Origin": "https://joblet.ai",
    })
    with urllib.request.urlopen(req, timeout=30) as r:
        return json.load(r)["data"]

# crawl /api/jobs remote with cursor, keep only Remote + tech categories
params = {"employmentType": "Remote", "limit": 50}
cursor = None
seen=set(); kept=[]; pages=0
while pages < 20:
    p=dict(params)
    if cursor: p["cursor"]=cursor
    data=fetch_jobs(p)
    pages += 1
    jobs=data.get("jobs") or []
    pag=data.get("pagination") or {}
    new=0
    for j in jobs:
        jid=j.get("id")
        if jid in seen: continue
        seen.add(jid); new+=1
        ets=[e.lower() for e in (j.get("employmentType") or [])]
        if "remote" not in ets: continue
        cat=(j.get("category") or "")
        if cat not in ("Technology & IT", "AI"): continue
        kept.append(j)
    print(f"p={pages} batch={len(jobs)} new={new} kept_total={len(kept)} hasMore={pag.get('hasMore')}")
    cursor=pag.get("nextCursor")
    if not pag.get("hasMore") or not cursor or new==0:
        break

print("kept", len(kept))
print("employers", Counter((j.get("company") or {}).get("name") for j in kept).most_common(15))
for j in kept[:12]:
    print("-", j["title"], "|", (j.get("company") or {}).get("name"), "|", j.get("location"), "|", j.get("applyUrl","")[:70])
PY
input.description: Crawl joblet remote tech jobs via cursor

python3 <<'PY'
import json, urllib.request, urllib.parse, re
from collections import Counter

# sitemap
req = urllib.request.Request('https://joblet.ai/sitemap.xml', headers={'User-Agent':'Mozilla/5.0'})
with urllib.request.urlopen(req, timeout=30) as r:
    xml = r.read().decode('utf-8', errors='replace')
print('sitemap len', len(xml))
print(xml[:2000])
locs = re.findall(r'<loc>([^<]+)</loc>', xml)
print('locs', len(locs))
print('sample', locs[:20])
print('job locs', [u for u in locs if '/job' in u.lower()][:20])

# try search with location=Remote
def get(path, params):
    q = urllib.parse.urlencode(params, doseq=True)
    url = f'https://joblet.ai{path}?{q}'
    req = urllib.request.Request(url, headers={
        'Accept':'application/json','User-Agent':'Mozilla/5.0',
        'Referer':'https://joblet.ai/jobs?employmentType=Remote','Origin':'https://joblet.ai',
    })
    with urllib.request.urlopen(req, timeout=30) as r:
        return json.load(r)

for params in [
    {'q':'software engineer','location':'Remote'},
    {'q':'software engineer','location':'remote'},
    {'q':'backend','employmentType':'Remote','location':'Worldwide'},
    {'q':'typescript','filters[employmentType]':'Remote'},
]:
    try:
        body = get('/api/search', params)
        data = body.get('data') or {}
        jobs = data.get('jobs') or []
        print('search', params, 'n=', len(jobs), 'meta=', {k:v for k,v in data.items() if k!='jobs'})
        if jobs:
            print(' ', jobs[0].get('title'), jobs[0].get('employmentType'), jobs[0].get('location'), (jobs[0].get('company') or {}).get('name'))
            print('  keys', sorted(jobs[0].keys())[:30])
            # check isRemote
            print('  isRemote?', jobs[0].get('isRemote'), jobs[0].get('is_remote'))
    except Exception as e:
        print('ERR', params, e)

# inspect jobs/search page1 apply hosts
body = get('/api/jobs/search', {'employmentType':'Remote','page':1})
jobs = body['data']['jobs']
hosts = Counter()
for j in jobs:
    u = j.get('applyUrl') or ''
    m = re.match(r'https?://([^/]+)/', u)
    hosts[m.group(1) if m else '(none)'] += 1
print('page1 apply hosts', hosts)
print('page1 samples:')
for j in jobs[:5]:
    print('-', j['title'], '|', (j.get('company') or {}).get('name'), '|', j.get('location'), '|', j.get('category'))
PY

python3 <<'PY'
import json, urllib.request, urllib.parse
from collections import Counter

QUERIES = [
    "software engineer", "backend", "frontend", "full stack", "devops",
    "platform engineer", "data engineer", "machine learning", "typescript",
    "python engineer", "golang", "staff engineer", "principal engineer",
    "SRE", "infrastructure", "android", "iOS engineer", "react",
]

def search(q, page=1):
    params = {"q": q, "location": "Remote", "page": page, "limit": 50}
    url = "https://joblet.ai/api/search?" + urllib.parse.urlencode(params)
    req = urllib.request.Request(url, headers={
        "Accept": "application/json", "User-Agent": "Mozilla/5.0",
        "Referer": "https://joblet.ai/jobs?employmentType=Remote",
        "Origin": "https://joblet.ai",
    })
    with urllib.request.urlopen(req, timeout=30) as r:
        return json.load(r)["data"]

seen = {}
for q in QUERIES:
    data = search(q)
    jobs = data.get("jobs") or []
    pag = data.get("pagination") or {}
    remote = [j for j in jobs if j.get("isRemote") or "remote" in (j.get("location") or "").lower()]
    print(f"q={q!r:30} n={len(jobs):2} remoteish={len(remote):2} total={pag.get('total')} sample={jobs[0]['title'] if jobs else '-'}")
    for j in remote:
        seen[j["id"]] = j
    # page 2 if has more
    if pag.get("hasMore") and pag.get("totalPages", 1) > 1:
        data2 = search(q, page=2)
        for j in data2.get("jobs") or []:
            if j.get("isRemote") or "remote" in (j.get("location") or "").lower():
                seen[j["id"]] = j

print("\nUNIQUE remoteish", len(seen))
print("employers", Counter((j.get("company") or {}).get("name") if isinstance(j.get("company"), dict) else j.get("company") for j in seen.values()).most_common(20))
print("categories", Counter(j.get("category") for j in seen.values()))
# apply url hosts
hosts=Counter()
for j in seen.values():
    u=j.get("applyUrl") or ""
    hosts[urllib.parse.urlparse(u).netloc] += 1
print("hosts", hosts)
for j in list(seen.values())[:15]:
    co = j.get("company")
    name = co.get("name") if isinstance(co, dict) else co
    print("-", j["title"], "|", name, "|", j.get("location"), "|", j.get("slug") or j.get("id"))
PY
input.description: Probe search API with tech queries

python3 <<'PY'
import urllib.request, re, json
url='https://joblet.ai/sitemap-jobs/1'
req=urllib.request.Request(url, headers={'User-Agent':'Mozilla/5.0'})
with urllib.request.urlopen(req, timeout=30) as r:
    xml=r.read().decode('utf-8','replace')
print(xml[:2500])
locs=re.findall(r'<loc>([^<]+)</loc>', xml)
print('n', len(locs))
print('samples', locs[:10])
PY
# one search result with slug
python3 <<'PY'
import json,urllib.request,urllib.parse
params={'q':'frontend','location':'Remote'}
url='https://joblet.ai/api/search?'+urllib.parse.urlencode(params)
req=urllib.request.Request(url,headers={'Accept':'application/json','User-Agent':'Mozilla/5.0','Origin':'https://joblet.ai','Referer':'https://joblet.ai/jobs'})
with urllib.request.urlopen(req,timeout=30) as r:
    jobs=json.load(r)['data']['jobs']
for j in jobs[:3]:
    print(json.dumps({k:j.get(k) for k in ['id','slug','title','isRemote','location','applyUrl','company','employmentType']}, indent=2))
PY

Build UI

", "applyUrl": "https://tnl2.jometer.com/v2/job?x=1", }, { "id": "2", "title": "Office Manager", "slug": "office-manager-local-xyz", "isRemote": False, "location": "New York, NY", "employmentType": ["On-site"], "company": {"name": "LocalCo"}, "applyUrl": "https://example.com/office", }, { "id": "3", "title": "DevOps Engineer (Remote)", "url_slug": None, "isRemote": False, "location": "Berlin, Germany", "employmentType": ["Remote"], "company": {"name": "Orbit"}, "applyUrl": "https://example.com/devops", }, { "id": "4", "title": "Missing employer", "isRemote": True, "location": "Remote", "employmentType": ["Remote"], "company": {"name": ""}, "applyUrl": "https://example.com/missing", }, ] }, } def test_joblet_board_url_defaults_and_preserves_host(): assert joblet_board_url("") == "https://joblet.ai/jobs?employmentType=Remote" assert ( joblet_board_url("https://joblet.ai/jobs") == "https://joblet.ai/jobs?employmentType=Remote" ) assert ( joblet_board_url("https://joblet.ai/jobs?employmentType=Remote") == "https://joblet.ai/jobs?employmentType=Remote" ) assert joblet_board_url("https://example.com/x") == ( "https://joblet.ai/jobs?employmentType=Remote" ) def test_parse_joblet_search_payload_keeps_remote_with_employer(): jobs = parse_joblet_search_payload(SAMPLE_PAYLOAD) assert len(jobs) == 2 by_title = {j["title"]: j for j in jobs} assert by_title["Senior Frontend Developer"]["employer"] == "Acme Labs" assert by_title["Senior Frontend Developer"]["url"] == ( "https://joblet.ai/jobs/senior-frontend-developer-acme-abc123" ) assert "Build UI" in by_title["Senior Frontend Developer"]["description_text"] assert by_title["DevOps Engineer (Remote)"]["employer"] == "Orbit" assert by_title["DevOps Engineer (Remote)"]["url"] == "https://example.com/devops"

content.text:
Added **Joblet** as a third remote aggregator board (`remote-joblet`), same pattern as Remote OK / Remote DXB.

**What shipped**
- Seed + ATS type `joblet` pointing at [joblet.ai remote jobs](https://joblet.ai/jobs?employmentType=Remote)
- Scraper in `relocation_jobs/scrape/boards/joblet.py`
- Wired into board dispatch, aggregator sync, and remote country list

**Fetch approach** — Their browse API (`/api/jobs?employmentType=Remote`) is noisy (lots of non-remote / CPA spam). The scraper uses `/api/search` with tech queries + `location=Remote`, then keeps remote rows and fans employers out like the other aggregators.

Restart the panel (or wait for seed bootstrap) so `ensure_aggregator_seeds()` creates the Joblet source; then fetch `remote-joblet` to pull listings.
